Level Design · Environmental Storytelling · AI Co-Design

Unspoken Laws.

A first-person narrative-puzzle prototype investigating how architectural principles — cognitive mapping, atmosphere, spatial composition — communicate emotional meaning in a void realm, built with AI as co-designer.

The Realm
Spatial Sequence
The Laws
The Void

01 — Concept

A void realm built
from architectural language.

Unspoken Laws invites the player into an abstract void representing the unspoken emotional rules we live by. Through environmental cues and architectural puzzles — with minimal UI and no text — the player decodes laws that reshape the realm around them.

The research question driving this project:

“In what ways can architectural principles be combined with human-assisted AI content generation tools to communicate environmental storytelling that conveys narrative meaning?”

Architecture is used metaphorically as a living language. Spatial composition, cognitive mapping, and phenomenological atmosphere are not decorative — they are the grammar through which the narrative speaks.

Pillar 01
Architecture as narrative medium
Cognitive mapping (Lynch), spatial composition (Totten), architectural phenomenology (Nitsche) — applied directly to level design decisions.
Pillar 02
Environmental storytelling + embodied perception
Jenkins’ embedded and emergent narrative, Austin’s role of space in engagement, Zumthor’s atmospheres — translated into spatial experience design.
Pillar 03
Human-AI co-design
Meshy AI, Leonardo AI, Eleven Labs, Adobe Firefly as co-designers — documenting what works, what fails, and the irreplaceable role of the human curator.
Entry Sequence
Brutalist corridor —
shadow as spatial language

02 — Design Process

How the space
was built.

01
Spatial &
Architectural Decisions

Each area of the void was designed using cognitive mapping to guide navigation without a minimap — landmarks, spatial sequences, and threshold moments replace traditional UI wayfinding.

The brutalist entry corridor uses dramatic shadow striping and compressed spatial ratios — architectural tension that communicates constraint before the player consciously reads it.

Zumthor’s atmospheres concept informed every material, light, and scale decision: the void floor as infinite horizon, electric blue as the realm’s emotional temperature.

Unity 2022Cognitive mappingAtmospheresSpatial composition
The Laws — spatial narrative without text
Entry corridor — compressed ratio, shadow striping
02
Environmental
Storytelling

The void communicates entirely through spatial and environmental cues — the triangle-eye symbol recurs as a narrative anchor, law fragments are distributed to reward exploration.

The colour palette is the emotional system: blue signals agency, gold signals discovery, white geometry signals constructed law, dark void signals the unknown.

Following Jenkins, the space holds both embedded narrative (history encoded in architecture) and emergent narrative (built through the player’s choices).

Jenkins (2004)Embodied perceptionEmergent narrative
03
AI Co-Design
Process

Meshy AI generated 3D environmental assets — crystals, organic forms, architectural fragments. Each output required curation: ~60% discarded, ~30% used as-is, ~10% modified post-import.

Leonardo AI produced concept images and skybox references. Eleven Labs generated ambient voices. Adobe Firefly handled texture variations and concept exploration.

Key finding: AI operates best as a rapid ideation partner. Every successful element required a human decision about narrative intent, placement, and tonal fit.

Meshy AILeonardo AIEleven LabsAdobe FireflyMidjourney
Meshy AI Draft selection — 4 geometry variants from a single prompt
Meshy AI Crystal arch with sigil — used as realm anchor object
Meshy AI Crystal obelisk cluster — environmental asset integration
Convai AI NPC dialogue integration attempt — ultimately unsuccessful due to tool constraints
Meshy AI “Glowing Rune Triangle” — selected variant after curation
The Void Realm
Environmental storytelling
through spatial cues

03 — Gameplay

Watch the
playthrough.

04 — Outcomes & Reflection

What worked.
What didn’t.

✓  What worked
Cognitive mapping as navigation
Players explored without a minimap. Spatial landmarks and threshold moments guided movement — architectural wayfinding worked.
Colour as emotional system
The blue/gold/void palette consistently communicated agency, discovery, and the unknown without text.
AI as ideation accelerator
Meshy AI and Leonardo AI compressed the asset creation timeline and opened directions that wouldn’t have emerged from manual modelling alone.
Atmosphere through scale contrast
The shift from the compressed entry corridor to the open void created a genuine phenomenological response.
—  Challenges
AI visual consistency
Maintaining coherence across multiple AI-generated assets was harder than expected. A centralised visual brief would have reduced curation time significantly.
Puzzle legibility
Some architectural puzzles required more visual signposting than the minimal-UI approach allowed.
Scope management
Research and prototype ambitions exceeded a single semester. A narrower, more deeply developed single realm would have been stronger.
Convai AI NPC integration
Attempted AI-driven NPC dialogue was ultimately unsuccessful — the tool’s constraints conflicted with the game’s minimal-language design philosophy.

Key design takeaways

Architecture’s spatial grammar is directly transferable to level design — not as metaphor but as functional tool.
AI co-design requires strong human design intent. The clearer the brief, the better the output.
Colour, scale, and light carry narrative weight that text cannot — bypassing conscious reading.
The designer’s curation role becomes more critical, not less, when AI tools are in the pipeline.

Enter thevoid realm.

First-person, browser-playable, no download required.

05 — AI Lab

Experiments &
explorations.

An ongoing space for AI-assisted design experiments, prompt documentation, and critical reflection on generative tools in spatial and game design contexts.

Experiment 01 · 2025
Unspoken Laws — AI Co-Design Log
Documented prompt-by-prompt exploration of Meshy AI, Leonardo AI, Eleven Labs, and Adobe Firefly as environmental design tools. Includes successful generations, failed attempts, and critical reflection on the human-AI design relationship. Key finding: stylistic consistency across AI tools requires a centralised visual reference system — something most current AI pipelines lack.
Meshy AILeonardo AIEleven LabsAdobe FireflyConvai AI (unsuccessful)
More experiments in progress — architectural render enhancement · AI-generated spatial concepts · real-time environment generation

06 — About

Spatial designer.
Both worlds.

Eight years of architectural and interior design practice — across Brazil and London, delivering high-end residential projects — form the foundation for a practice that moves between physical and digital space.

MA research at Brunel University (2025, on track for Distinction) formalised what practice had long demonstrated: that spatial design and level design share the same fundamental grammar. Cognitive mapping, atmosphere, embodied perception — these are architectural concepts that operate identically in physical and digital environments.

The work sits at the intersection of spatial narrative, game engines, and AI-assisted design — a combination that is increasingly rare and increasingly valuable across architecture, immersive experience, and interactive media.
